sanskrit: पश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहे सर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान्। ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थमृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान्॥
transliteration: paśyāmi devāṃs tava deva dehe sarvāṃs tathā bhūtaviśeṣasaṅghān | brahmāṇam īśaṃ kamalāsanastham ṛṣīṃś ca sarvān uragāṃś ca divyān ||
literal_meaning: I see the gods in Your body, O Lord, and likewise all the particular hosts of beings; Brahmā, the lord, seated on the lotus, and all the seers and the divine serpents.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s perceptual field suddenly expands into a panoramic vision of the entire cosmos. Every significant figure—creator, destroyer, wisdom-keepers, and primordial forces—appears contained within one luminous form. The psyche is momentarily relieved of its usual filters, revealing that all inner “characters” (archetypes) coexist in a single Self.
psychological_state: Transient ego-inflation followed by ego-catastrophe; the mind confronts the archetypal pantheon and must surrender its claim to centrality.
trigger_scenario: During a psychedelic-assisted therapy session, the client sees every significant person and symbol from their life arrayed inside a single mandala-like image, realizing “it’s all me.”
anchor_text: Every inner figure lives inside you; meet them as one Self.
